segments: Wizards of the Coast and Digital Gaming

supply_chain.dependencies: Most of our toy and game products are manufactured by third-party manufacturers, the majority of which are in China, with a significant amount of our product sourcing also coming from manufacturers in the U.S., Vietnam, India and Japan
risks: Reliance on third-party manufacturers presents risks to our business
Tariffs and trade sanctions could significantly increase cost of products
Natural disasters or health pandemics impacting manufacturers
Port congestion, strikes, labor shortages causing transportation delays
Suspension of operations by government inspectors could result in delays

regulatory_risks: Children's Television Act and FCC regulations
Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) compliance
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in European Union
Data protection laws in United States and other countries
New laws and regulations governing artificial intelligence
Product safety and consumer protection statutes in international markets
Restrictions on chemicals including phthalates and BPA

sanction_risks: Imposition or threat of tariffs, including reciprocal or retaliatory tariffs
Trade sanctions, quotas, border adjustment taxes or other protectionist measures
Loss of normal trade relations status with China or other foreign countries
Political instability, civil unrest and economic instability in international markets
